other_observers: Chris Wood, Larry Semo, Tom Schultz; Tom Schultz originally identified the bird.
specific_location: in marsh west of 16 Road, south of JJ Road (currently -- Jan 2006 -- not wet and hasn't been for a few years)
description: We never saw the bird. While conducting a Big Day with Chris Wood, Larry Semo, and Tom Schultz, we were listening to the marsh for nocturnal marsh birds. There were numerous Virginia Rails, Soras, and Black Rails calling and we were waiting for American Bittern, when Tom said something like, "Hey, isn't that King Rail." We all immediately agreed, as we'd all heard the bird, which, over the next few minutes, gave the call two more times.  Note: The website will not let me include a question mark in this field, so the Tom quote, which should have an ending question mark, has a period instead.Behaviors: 'Singing' from marshCall: The bird gave the "male advertising call" at least three times -- a longish series of relatively low-pitched, hollow 'kek' notes (>9, <15 individual notes) with very similar inter-note intervals throughout the 'song.'Plumage: unknown
